mappings.rationale: Examines the Paris Peace Conference aftermath, the establishment of the League of Nations, and enforcement crises directly discussed in A.1.b.
Provides essential context for the post-WWI diplomatic landscape, the Locarno agreements, and the peace settlement problems in A.1-2.

mappings.vsiTitle: The Great Depression and The New Deal
mappings.rationale: Traces the Great Depression's collapse of the world market and the New Deal policy response in the U.S. discussed in A.4.b-c.
Directly addresses the Popular Front and Spanish Civil War (1936-39) including German and Italian intervention discussed in A.3.b.
Traces the rise of fascism from Mussolini's Italian aggression against Ethiopia to the formation of the Axis alliance discussed in A.3-4 and B.
Directly addresses the establishment of the Third Reich, rearmament-based economic recovery, and Germany's alignment with Italy and Japan in A.4.c.
Comprehensive treatment of WWII including German conquests, the Pacific war, Allied strategy, and Axis defeat discussed throughout B.
Examines developments in military aviation including the Battle of Britain and strategic bombing relevant to B.1 and B.6.
Directly addresses the burgeoning of military technology including communications, naval vessels, aircraft, missiles, and atomic bombs in B.6.
Atomic and hydrogen bombs, the balance of terror, France as a nuclear power, and the arms buildup are all discussed in B.6, C.2.a, and C.2.f.
Directly addresses the Iron Curtain, Berlin blockade, NATO formation, containment, the Cuban Missile Crisis, detente, and the end of the Cold War in C.2-3.
Traces Soviet diplomatic isolation, the Nazi-Soviet pact, Soviet expansion into eastern Europe, de-Stalinization, and USSR disintegration across A-C.
Explores the establishment and evolving role of the UN, peacekeeping operations, and the Security Council discussed in C.2.a and throughout the postwar section.
Traces diplomatic history from the Treaty of Versailles through Cold War negotiations and detente, relevant throughout A-C.
Examines European integration movements and economic recovery in western Europe discussed in C.3.b.ii.
Explores decolonization, demands for self-determination among subject peoples, and the emergence of new states in A.3.c and C.3.a.ii.
Addresses the Palestine question, the founding of Israel, and the Arab-Israeli wars discussed in C.2.e and C.3.c.ii.
Examines the Iran-Iraq War and Persian Gulf wars directly discussed in C.3.c.ii.
Explores Soviet expansion, consolidation of communist power in eastern Europe, and the ideological dimension of the Cold War throughout C.
International terrorism as a post-Cold War area of conflict is directly discussed in C.3.c.iv.
Examines Japanese militarism, the Greater East Asia Co-prosperity Sphere, and Japan's role in the Pacific war discussed in A.3.a.
Addresses civil conflict in China and communist rule relevant to A.3.a (Manchuria, civil war) and C.2.d (Korean War context).
Explores Chinese communist rule and Sino-Soviet relations relevant to C.2.d.ii and C.3.a.viii.
Nationalist movements drove demands for self-determination among colonial subject peoples in A.3.c and the postwar period.
Examines NATO, Cold War security architecture, the balance of terror, and post-Cold War security challenges in C.2-3.
Addresses the international system, state sovereignty, and institutions relevant to the section's broad treatment of diplomacy and war since 1920.
The Holocaust and concentration camp system were a central feature of WWII discussed in B.
Analyzes strategic plans, goals, and tactical procedures of the warring powers discussed in B.4-5.
Traces the evolution of modern warfare from the Spanish Civil War through WWII and Cold War conflicts including Korea and Vietnam.
Examines civil wars including the Spanish Civil War, Chinese civil war, and sub-Saharan African civil conflicts in A.3.b, C.2.d, and C.3.c.iii.
Explores postwar European recovery, Cold War division, and the end of European empires relevant to C.2 and C.3.b.
Examines dictatorships in interwar central and eastern Europe and Soviet-style authoritarian regimes discussed in A.1.a and C.3.b.iii.
Massive displacement during WWII and post-colonial conflicts, including in Sub-Saharan Africa, is discussed in B and C.3.c.iii.
Iran's role in Middle East conflicts, including the Iran-Iraq War, is discussed in C.3.c.ii.
Explores geopolitical dynamics of Cold War spheres of influence and post-Cold War reconfiguration throughout C.
Explores peacekeeping, the League of Nations, the Kellogg-Briand agreement, and the Helsinki conference discussed in A.2 and C.3.a.x.
Addresses humanitarian intervention, refugees, and peacebuilding relevant to international movements and post-Cold War conflicts in C.3.c.
